title: Velvety Argentinian Freekeh Pilaf
description: A buttery, velvety pilaf featuring toasted freekeh, aromatic spices, and a creamy cashew- almond sauce, perfect for a solo, jain-friendly dinner.

ingredients.name: cashew butter
ingredients.notes: soak cashews 30 min, blend with 1 Tbsp water to create smooth cream

instructions: Rinse the freekeh and toast it in a dry skillet over medium heat until lightly browned, about 2–3 minutes; set aside.
Heat the olive oil in the same skillet, then add cumin, smoked paprika, and chili flakes; cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Pour a splash of the warm vegetable broth to deglaze the pan, scraping up all the browned bits.
Add the toasted freekeh to the skillet and pour in the remaining broth. Bring to a gentle simmer, then cover and reduce heat to low. Cook for 15 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the skillet from the heat and whisk in the warm almond milk, cashew cream, and lemon juice until the mixture is smooth and velvety.
Season the pilaf with salt and pepper to taste, stirring well.
Divide the pilaf between two plates, garnish with chopped parsley, cilantro, and toasted almonds.
Serve immediately while hot, enjoying the buttery, velvety texture.
